commit.message:
chore: remove workaround for dotnet/sdk#52160

The .NET SDK fix (dotnet/sdk#52161) is available since SDK 10.0.105.
The workaround in PublishChiseledContainer that manually appended
'-extra' to ContainerFamily when InvariantGlobalization=false is no
longer needed - the SDK now handles this correctly.

UpdateContainerBaseImage now extracts the container family from the
already-computed ContainerBaseImage tag instead of using the raw
ContainerFamily property. This ensures CustomContainerBaseImageVersion
preserves the correct family suffix (e.g. noble-chiseled-extra) as
resolved by the SDK, since the SDK task only outputs ContainerBaseImage
and does not update ContainerFamily.

Test updated to explicitly set InvariantGlobalization=true for the
non-extra variant, reflecting the SDK's new default behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: auto-remove legacy flowsmartly-apps.conf on nginx regenerate

The old V2 nginx-config wrote location blocks to conf.d/flowsmartly-apps.conf
which is invalid (location directives require server context). V3 now writes
to flowsmartly-upstreams.conf + flowsmartly-locations/ dir. This cleanup
ensures the legacy file is deleted whenever regenerateAndReload() runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix nullable description in ModelSummary causing empty models list

Backend always returns description: null but Zod schema used
z.string().optional() which rejects null values, throwing at parse
time and leaving availableModels as [].

- contracts: mark ModelSummary.description as nullable: true
- frontend schemas: z.string().optional() → z.string().nullish()
- frontend types: description?: string → description?: string | null

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(formatter): semicolons on own line, AS alignment, graceful parse errors (#1)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Three formatter style fixes from real-world usage feedback.

### Trailing semicolons on their own line
Previously `;` was appended to the last line of each statement. Now it
always appears as a standalone line.

### Column alias AS alignment
When a SELECT has 2+ aliased columns (all single-line), the `AS`
keywords are padded to align vertically:

```sql
SELECT
   first_name    AS first
  ,last_name     AS last
  ,email_address AS email
FROM t
```

Multi-line column expressions (e.g. `CASE`) disable alignment for the
whole SELECT.

### Graceful handling of parse failures
`format_sql` now returns `(formatted_sql, warnings)` instead of
crashing. On `ParseError`, the original SQL is returned unchanged with a
warning. This partially addresses #2 (`PIVOT ... USING ... ORDER BY ...`
not yet supported by sqlglot) — the formatter no longer crashes; a
proper fix is tracked separately.

## Changes

- `formatter.py` — returns `(str, list[FormatWarning])` tuple; catches
`ParseError` gracefully
- `generator.py` — adds `alias_sql` override with `_as_align_width`
state for AS alignment
- `cli.py` — unpacks the new tuple, prints warnings as `WARN` instead of
crashing
- All callers updated to unpack the tuple
- 4 new tests; 44/44 passing
- `tmp/` added to `.gitignore`

Resolves #2

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add memory profiling; comprehensive accuracy/speed/memory comparison in PDF

Memory measurement:
- CPU RAM: peak RSS delta sampled at 50ms via psutil (threading)
- GPU VRAM: torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated() delta
- Results stored in metrics.json per scene per method

Key findings:
- VGGT:  +7.8-9.7 GB RAM (model loading), 10.4-12.8 GB VRAM
- SfM:   +9-19 MB RAM, 0 VRAM (pure CPU)
- PHR:   +27-53 MB RAM (BA only), 0 VRAM (BA stage CPU-only)

Report updates:
- New memory figure (CPU RAM + GPU VRAM side-by-side bar charts)
- New comprehensive summary table (accuracy + speed + memory all in one)
- New trade-off summary table in conclusion
- Memory requirements section with detailed discussion
- Fixed LaTeX: degree symbol replaced with \$LASTEXITCODE\circ\$ for pdflatex compat
- Fixed compile_pdf to not crash when stdout is None (binary mode)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

After pulling new commits, the update check now:

commit.message:
feat: distinguish 'waiting for game' from server errors (#37)

* Initial plan

* feat: distinguish 'waiting for game' from server errors

Categorize FETCH_ERROR by error type:
- ECONNREFUSED/ECONNRESET → game_not_running (waiting for game)
- Certificate errors → cert_error (config problem)
- Other errors → unknown

Only game_not_running resets to WAITING_FOR_GAME. Other errors
surface to the hub UI with an error state (red styling + reason).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Ilia01/Beacon/sessions/3c40a88e-4902-4fbb-b2da-880452de9dc1

Co-authored-by: Ilia01 <155351862+Ilia01@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: reset prompt state and window visibility on non-game errors

Error transitions now call resetState() in handleServerMessage and
stopPromptLoop()/overlay.hide()/hub.show() in main.ts, ensuring
cooldown timers, prompt history, and window visibility are properly
cleaned up when cert or unknown errors occur mid-game.

commit.message:
refactor: eliminate parameter mutation in context/detector helpers (#35)

* Initial plan

* refactor: return state changes instead of mutating newState parameter

- processEvents now returns { newEvents, stateUpdates } instead of mutating newState
- detectVision and detectTabCheck return stateUpdates via DetectorResult
- detectItemCompleted no longer mutates newState; item tracking moved to deriveContext
- Removed newState from DetectorInput type
- deriveContext merges all state updates from processEvents and detectors
- Added DetectorResult.stateUpdates optional field
- Exported getRealItems for use in deriveContext
- Added 3 immutability tests verifying state parameter is never mutated

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Ilia01/Beacon/sessions/f01206eb-225b-423f-87d8-40450536f3d4

Co-authored-by: Ilia01 <155351862+Ilia01@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: use deepFreeze and structuredClone in immutability tests

Shallow copy ({ ...state }) wouldn't catch mutations to nested arrays
like lastMyItemIds. Now deep-freeze the input state (so any mutation
throws) and use structuredClone for the comparison snapshot.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Ilia01/Beacon/sessions/b5f93a2a-cf0e-4c5b-9297-1bad73959eff

Co-authored-by: Ilia01 <155351862+Ilia01@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Protect innerText invariant in renderer with regression test (#36)

* Initial plan

* fix: add sanitizeText guard for prompt text in renderer.js

Defense-in-depth: strip HTML from prompt strings via DOMParser
before DOM insertion so future changes to innerHTML won't
introduce XSS. Also guards against null/undefined input.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Ilia01/Beacon/sessions/4258d419-9607-411a-953a-1078f5aa6e8d

Co-authored-by: Ilia01 <155351862+Ilia01@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rework: revert sanitizeText, add invariant test instead

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Ilia01/Beacon/sessions/78c779e4-6dd9-4ec6-b714-cd3064fe585f

Co-authored-by: Ilia01 <155351862+Ilia01@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add renderer invariant test for innerText safety

Adds a static-analysis test (renderer.test.ts) that reads renderer.js
and asserts prompt text is set via innerText, not innerHTML.  This
protects against accidental XSS regression without mutating display text
at runtime.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Ilia01/Beacon/sessions/78c779e4-6dd9-4ec6-b714-cd3064fe585f

commit.message:
feat: add admin PUT rebuild endpoint for store redeploy

PUT /api/admin/migrate-stores-v3?storeId=xxx triggers buildStoreV3+deployStoreV3
without re-running the AI agent (cheaper, for deploy-failed stores).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Content Editor: AI-powered suggestions, drafts, and content plans

New 'Content Editor' view in the sidebar with three capabilities:

1. **New Content suggestions** — identifies uncovered documentation topics
   grouped by category, with priority badges and topic counts
2. **Update recommendations** — flags partially-matched modules that need
   content expansion, showing match scores
3. **Outdated module detection** — surfaces modules >12 months old that
   need refreshing

Each suggestion has a 'Generate Draft' button that uses GitHub Models AI to
produce:
- For new modules: complete index.yml draft with title, summary, learning
  objectives, units, prerequisites, and suggested repo placement
- For updates: specific content changes, new units, and revised objectives
- For outdated: staleness assessment, rewrite plan, and refresh checklist

Additional features:
- 'Content Plan' button generates a full prioritized plan (immediate /
  short-term / long-term) exportable as Markdown
- Draft preview panel with markdown rendering
- Export drafts as YAML, Markdown, or JSON
- Copy to clipboard support
- Auto-builds suggestions when navigating to editor view

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Data-Structure
repository.description: 这是一个关于数据结构的项目，重点研究数组、链表、栈、队列、树和图等常见数据结构的设计、实现与应用。其目的是加深对数据结构基本概念的理解，掌握各类数据结构的核心操作（插入、删除、查找和遍历），并运用相关知识解决实际编程问题。

Updated Package.swift (dependency URL and product name), changed import in Sources/Track.swift, and modified Mixpanel.xcodeproj entries to reference MixpanelSwiftCommon (XCRemoteSwiftPackageReference, XCSwiftPackageProductDependency, framework entries and groups). Also bumped the package requirement to upToNextMajor with minimumVersion 2.0.0.

* Lower mixpanel-swift-common min version and remove duplicate

commit.message:
feat: Epic 5 — Active Workout Page redesign

E5-T1: Sticky timer topbar — breadcrumb (← Workouts), large monospace
  accent-coloured timer centred, Complete button pinned right.
  Sits below the mobile fixed topbar via top: var(--topbar-height);
  drops to top: 0 on desktop (≥768 px). Backdrop blur for glass effect.

E5-T2: Redesign exercise cards — dark .card surface, exercise name
  prominent with sets-count badge; set rows replace the <table>:
  each row shows set #, weight (kg), reps, and icon buttons.

E5-T3: Animate set rows — TransitionGroup name=list for slide-in on
  add; done sets get accent-filled circle checkmark + faded row
  background (no strikethrough).

E5-T4: Styled exercise select — native <select> with .input .select
  classes (custom chevron, appearance: none) inside a flex row.

E5-T5: Per-exercise collapse toggle — collapsedExercises Set<number>
  ref; chevron icon rotates -90 deg when collapsed; Transition
  name=collapse wraps the sets wrapper.

E5-T6: Inline confirms replace confirm() dialogs —
  confirmComplete (topbar) toggles between Complete button and a
  Finish? / Yes / No row (Transition name=fade mode=out-in);
  confirmRemoveId tracks which exercise shows Remove? / Yes / No.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add sparse matrix transpose teaching visualizer (part3)

Three static files for an interactive step-by-step algorithm visualizer:

- sparse_matrix_visualizer.html  — page structure (Chinese UI, 6 sections)
- sparse_matrix_visualizer.css   — clean light theme with colored highlights
- sparse_matrix_visualizer.js    — complete step generation + playback engine

Features:
* Visualizes all three C algorithms from part3/sparse_matrix.c:
  transpose_naive (O(cols×terms)), transpose_fast (O(cols+terms)),
  transpose_fast_optimized (O(cols+terms), single aux array)
* Steps generated fully upfront; play/pause/prev/next/reset + speed control
* 2×2 data grid: source 6×6 matrix, source triple table,
  result triple table (9-slot fixed), result 6×6 matrix
* Per-step highlights: scanned column, matched triple, written slot,
  num[]/cpot[] cell being updated
* Algorithm state panel shows live num[], cpot[], naive loop vars
* Complexity comparison table with speedup estimates

All three algorithms verified to produce identical correct result:
  (1,3,-3)(1,5,15)(2,1,12)(3,1,9)(3,4,24)(3,6,-4)(4,5,-7)(5,4,18)(6,3,14)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[development-pm-c361] Complete TASK-184: Launch planning strategy

Created comprehensive go-to-market launch plan for Phase 2 visual canvas release:

Launch Channels:
- Product Hunt (page setup, hunter outreach, launch day schedule)
- Hacker News (timing strategy, post format, engagement plan)
- Social media (Twitter/X 10-tweet thread, LinkedIn, Reddit 5 subreddits)
- Email outreach (warm network, TypeScript influencers, cold leads)
- Community (Discord, GitHub, developer relations)
- Press (dev/AI publications, press release template)

Deliverables:
- Master timeline (pre-launch week, launch day, 4-week growth)
- Content templates (PH First Comment, HN post, email templates)
- Success metrics (Week 1: 500+ stars, 200+ downloads; Week 4: 2K+ stars)
- Risk mitigation (technical issues, negative feedback, competitive response)
- Launch checklist (40+ action items across product, marketing, community)

Target: Launch Tuesday 12:01am PST after TASK-185 QA completion.

commit.message:
perf: lower MAX_MAGLEV_DEOPT_RETRIES to 3 for faster interpreter fallback

Functions that still deopt after the 200-iteration Maglev warmup phase have
persistent issues (e.g. prototype chain lookups that always trigger OVERFLOW).
Retrying 10,000 times just wastes time on deopt/recompile cycles. With limit 3,
such functions fall back to the interpreter within 3 deopt events, avoiding the
~600µs overhead of repeated deopt-recompile cycles.

Expected impact: prototype_chain 620.8µs → ~4µs (interpreter speed), BEATS V8.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore(castor): amélioration et extension des tâches Castor

- Root cs/analyse/test : ajout de platau en parallèle (couverture complète)
- prevarisc:start : --watch optionnel (ne bloque plus le terminal par défaut)
- prevarisc:rebuild : build + up après changement de config
- prevarisc:logs [service] : logs Docker suivis avec filtre service optionnel
- prevarisc:shell : shell interactif dans le container app
- symfony:cache : raccourci cache:clear
- symfony:make : wrapper make:* (make:entity, make:migration…)
- symfony:migration-status : état des migrations Doctrine
- symfony:status : git status en parallèle des deux repos
- symfony:validate : PHPStan + CS dry-run + tests séquentiels (fail-fast)
- worktree:list : tableau des worktrees actifs
- worktree:setup : supprimé (worktree:switch couvre ce cas)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
